quickPrompts.afghanVolunteers: Tell me about Afghan Red Crescent volunteers
quickPrompts.disasterResponse: Show me global disaster response data
quickPrompts.humanitarianIndicators: What are the key humanitarian indicators?

aiResponses.afghanRedCrescent: Over the past years, the Afghan Red Crescent Society (ARCS) has relied heavily on its volunteer network to respond to natural disasters, health crises, and conflict-related emergencies across Afghanistan. In 2013, ARCS reported having around 23,440 active volunteers (source), contributing to activities in nearly all provinces. A longitudinal review of ARCS's volunteer engagement efforts, supported by the Swedish Red Cross and ICRC, is detailed in a case study (source). Volunteers played crucial roles in major emergencies such as the 2015 avalanches, the 2022 Paktika earthquake, and the 2023 Herat earthquakes. According to the IFRC's Everyone Counts Report 2022, ARCS reported 393,077 volunteers contributing over 2.1 million hours of service, including 257,437 hours in health-related activities, with 56,456 people trained and 31,600 receiving first aid. These figures highlight the scale and impact of volunteerism in ARCS operations, though other sources suggest fluctuations in volunteer numbers over time.
aiResponses.disasterResponse: Humanitarian networks respond to hundreds of disasters and emergencies annually, providing critical assistance worldwide. In 2023, large-scale disaster response reached many millions of people affected by climate-related disasters, conflicts, and health emergencies. Key response areas include emergency shelter, food security, water and sanitation, health services, and psychosocial support. Response is often coordinated through emergency funds and appeals, ensuring rapid assistance to affected communities.
aiResponses.humanitarianIndicators: The databank tracks several key humanitarian indicators including volunteers, staff, local units, blood donors, first aid recipients, people reached, income, and expenditure. These indicators provide comprehensive insights into the network's capacity, reach, and impact across different regions and contexts. The data helps measure progress towards humanitarian goals and informs strategic decision-making for future operations.
aiResponses.regionalComparison: Regional comparison of volunteer numbers shows varying levels of engagement across different parts of the world. Africa and Asia typically have the highest volunteer numbers, reflecting the extensive community-based programs in these regions. Europe and the Americas show strong volunteer engagement in specific areas like disaster response and health services. The Middle East demonstrates growing volunteer participation, particularly in emergency response and community health initiatives.

meta.description: Build and test API endpoints for accessing databank data.

hero.title: API Endpoint Builder
hero.description: Build and test API endpoints to access databank data programmatically.

description.title: Data Access API
description.text: Use this tool to build API endpoints for accessing databank data. The data endpoint allows you to retrieve form submissions, indicators, and other data with various filters and parameters.
description.note: Note: This tool is for the public data endpoint only. For full API access, contact your administrator.

endpoint.title: Endpoint
endpoint.description: Retrieve data from databank forms and submissions (API key automatically included)

docs.title: API Documentation
docs.intro: Construct requests and learn how to use the Databank API effectively. Below are essentials for authentication, filtering, pagination, and disaggregation.
docs.authInstruction: Pass your API key via the {{param}} query parameter.
docs.exampleFilterPaginate: Filter by template, period, and paginate

quickStart.title: Quick Start Guide

quickStart.step3: Copy the generated URL or test it directly
quickStart.step4: Use the URL in your applications or scripts
